title: Learners and teachers excel at National Skills Competition
description: OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Western Cape learners and...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Western Cape learners and teachers have delivered an exceptional performance by winning 14 awards at the 2025 National Skills Competition, held in Johannesburg.","html":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - Western Cape learners and teachers have delivered an exceptional performance by winning 14 awards at the 2025 National Skills Competition, held in Johannesburg."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This national event was presented by the Sasol Foundation and the Kagiso Trust, and brought together learners, teachers and TVET College students from all nine provinces to showcase their technical and vocational talents across a wide range of disciplines."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Participants competed in real-world, industry-relevant tasks and Western Cape learners achieved five awards with one category win, while teachers achieved nine awards with five category wins.","html":"Participants competed in real-world, industry-relevant tasks and Western Cape learners achieved five awards with one category win, while teachers achieved nine awards with five category wins."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Learner awards","html":"Learner awards"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Kayden Chandler, a learner from Langenhoven Gimnasium, excelled in Power Systems and took second place.","html":"Kayden Chandler, a learner from Langenhoven Gimnasium, excelled in Power Systems and took second place."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This is an outstanding achievement and a testament to the hard work of the technical learners and teachers in the province. These skills are vital to the economy and to ensuring that learners are ready to move into further technical studies and employment after they matriculate.","html":"This is an outstanding achievement and a testament to the hard work of the technical learners and teachers in the province. These skills are vital to the economy and to ensuring that learners are ready to move into further technical studies and employment after they matriculate."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Nathan Joseph, Senior Curriculum Planner for Mechanical and Electrical Technology and lead organiser of the provincial competition, added his congratulations: “Our passionate and dedicated team of Western Cape learners and teachers gave their all.","html":"Nathan Joseph, Senior Curriculum Planner for Mechanical and Electrical Technology and lead organiser of the provincial competition, added his congratulations: “Our passionate and dedicated team of Western Cape learners and teachers gave their all."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"Their work was of high quality and reflected true commitment. As we celebrate National Teachers Month, we highlight the passion and dedication of our teachers in the technical fields."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"They inspire their learners to achieve in national competitions and beyond, because behind every dream realised is a teacher who believed. Thank you for the support you give to our learners every day here in the Western Cape.”","html":"\"They inspire their learners to achieve in national competitions and beyond, because behind every dream realised is a teacher who believed. Thank you for the support you give to our learners every day here in the Western Cape.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’","html":"‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’"}]

images.alt: Learners and teachers excel at National Skills Competition
